briefing.headline: Rig up a Drop Shot — finesse summer approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Drop Shot is a strong choice for summer with muddy water and post frontal conditions.

Johnston: On the St. Lawrence, fish an 80-yard stretch of bottom in about 40 feet targeting a pod of giant smallmouth. Catch one, then leave the spot and make a big run away before returning, because pressured deepwater smallmouth shut down after each catch.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, dock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, dock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Palaniuk: 'My offshore rotation goes: 1) deep-diving crankbait to cover water fast and fire up the school, 2) swimbait for a slower moving presentation once crankbait bite dies, 3) big flutter spoon for vertical reaction bites on fish seen on elec...

KVD: 'Summer bass suspend when temps peak, no current, and thermocline forms. See the thermocline as a consistent band on your graph — fish above it, none below. Schooling activity away from bottom and structure is a giveaway. Clouds of suspended ...

Johnston: On the Potomac, I found fish out in the grass, not in the real thick stuff. I was casting to catch them, not flipping. When that failed due to muddy water from rain, I moved to flipping grass on the Greenway Flats.
Wheeler: The thermocline sets up deeper (25-30 feet) in clear-water reservoirs and shallower (8-10 feet) in murky waters; rivers generally don't have consistent thermoclines due to flowing water

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal summer fish retreat to reliable offshore structure at 12-25ft. They're less aggressive but still need to eat. Focus on primary structure — main lake points, ledges, and humps with shade or current.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If you get followers without commits, add scent or downsize your hook/weight
